assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and a local project or empty directory.
No WorkOS account, product selection, or AuthKit framework route has been chosen yet.

developer_goal: Create a WorkOS account and reach the first documented success for a chosen WorkOS product and integration route.

first_success.outcome: The selected WorkOS product route reaches its documented first workflow result.
first_success.observable_signal: Depends on the unresolved product; for AuthKit Node.js, the local app can complete hosted sign-up and the user appears in the Dashboard Users list.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after one product and one integration surface are fixed.

candidates.name: AuthKit AI Installer
candidates.reason: Docs present npx workos install as the fastest AuthKit path, but it edits the project and still requires a framework and WorkOS account.

candidates.name: AuthKit manual framework guide
candidates.reason: Manual stack guides such as Node.js are peer alternatives to the AI Installer with different action shapes.

candidates.name: Non-AuthKit WorkOS products
candidates.reason: Docs expose additional product families such as SSO, Directory Sync, Vault, and Widgets with different first-success outcomes.

required_path.action: Create a WorkOS account and obtain API Key and Client ID for a staging environment.

required_fields.label: WorkOS account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Guides require a WorkOS account but do not publish the exact signup form fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can access the WorkOS Dashboard and copy API credentials.

required_path.action: Configure redirect URIs and related Dashboard settings required by the selected AuthKit or product route.

required_fields.label: Sign-in endpoint if required by AuthKit

required_path.observable_result: Dashboard redirect settings match the local application callbacks.

required_path.action: Integrate the selected product using the AI Installer or manual framework guide.
required_path.observable_result: The local app builds or runs with AuthKit or the selected product wired.

required_path.action: Execute the route's first user workflow, such as signing up through AuthKit.

required_fields.label: First user authentication inputs for the configured methods

required_path.observable_result: The product-specific success signal appears, such as a Dashboard user for AuthKit.

prerequisites.description: A WorkOS account with API Key and Client ID is required before AuthKit guides proceed.
AuthKit AI Installer requires Node.js 20+ and a supported framework project.

platform_outcomes.description: WorkOS issues API credentials and can auto-configure redirect settings when the CLI installer authenticates.
Successful AuthKit sign-up creates a user visible in the Dashboard Users section.

excluded.item: Production environment cutover and custom branding
excluded.reason: Staging versus production and branding guides come after the first AuthKit integration.

excluded.item: Reading the full product catalog as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation browsing is excluded from required developer actions.

sources.title: WorkOS Docs
evidence_supported: Multiple product families without one platform-wide default

uncertainties.question: Which WorkOS product and AuthKit installer versus manual framework route define the canonical first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Product choice changes the success outcome itself; installer versus manual changes required actions and trust boundaries.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded route such as AuthKit Next.js AI Installer, then verify account creation through first hosted sign-up.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields appear on current WorkOS account signup?
uncertainties.impact: Signup field counts remain unverified without a public or fresh-account capture.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of WorkOS Dashboard registration for a new developer.
